The Nile River has two major tributaries, the White Nile and the Blue Nile. The White Nile is generally considered to be the primary stream and its most distant source is located in Rwanda, although Lake Victoria in Uganda is often cited as a main source. The Blue Nile begins in Ethiopia at Lake Tana. Therefore, the Nile starts in multiple countries, but its most distant source is found in Rwanda, while significant sections begin in Uganda and Ethiopia.
